learning.whatWillYouLearn: Prerequisites: Arrays, Kadane's Algorithm, Loops, Dynamic Programming Basics, Time Complexity
learning.whyDoesThisExist: Imagine running around a circular race track collecting points. You may start anywhere and continue around the track, even wrapping from the end back to the beginning. Find the path with the maximum total score.
learning.realWorldAnalogy: Imagine running around a circular race track collecting points. You may start anywhere and continue around the track, even wrapping from the end back to the beginning. Find the path with the maximum total score.
learning.whenToUseThis: Use this pattern whenever the array is circular and the problem asks for a maximum contiguous subarray sum.
learning.signals: Whenever the array is circular and the problem asks for a maximum contiguous sum, think of Kadane's Algorithm combined with totalSum - minimumSubarray.
learning.expectedLearningOutcome: I will know when to use this pattern.

problem.problemStatement: Given a circular integer array nums of length n, return the maximum possible sum of a non-empty subarray of nums. A circular array means the end of the array connects to the beginning.

examples.explanation: The circular subarray [5,5] gives the maximum sum of 10.

thinkingProcess.naiveIdea: Generate every possible circular subarray and calculate its sum.
thinkingProcess.keyInsight: There are two possible answers: either the maximum subarray lies completely inside the array (normal Kadane), or it wraps around the ends. The wrapped sum equals totalSum minus the minimum subarray sum.
thinkingProcess.ahaMoment: A wrapped maximum subarray equals the total array sum minus the minimum subarray.
thinkingProcess.patternRecognition: Whenever the array is circular and the problem asks for a maximum contiguous sum, think of Kadane's Algorithm combined with totalSum - minimumSubarray.
thinkingProcess.whyItWorks: The maximum circular subarray either does not wrap (Kadane) or wraps around. Wrapping excludes the minimum subarray, so totalSum - minSum gives the wrapped maximum.
thinkingProcess.howToRecognizeSimilar: Whenever the array is circular and the problem asks for a maximum contiguous sum, think of Kadane's Algorithm combined with totalSum - minimumSubarray.
commonWrongPaths: Forgetting to calculate the minimum subarray.
Returning total-minSum when all numbers are negative.

hints: Solve the normal maximum subarray first.
Find the minimum subarray as well.
commonMisconceptions: Forgetting to calculate the minimum subarray.
Returning total-minSum when all numbers are negative.

pattern.name: Kadane + Circular Array
pattern.recognitionSignals: Whenever the array is circular and the problem asks for a maximum contiguous sum, think of Kadane's Algorithm combined with totalSum - minimumSubarray.

approaches.title: Check Every Circular Subarray
approaches.idea: Generate every possible circular subarray and calculate its sum.

code.java: Brute force omitted because of cubic complexity.

approaches.title: Prefix Sum
approaches.idea: Use prefix sums to compute circular subarray sums faster, but still requires checking many ranges.
approaches.dryRun: Compute every circular range using prefix sums.

approaches.title: Kadane + Circular Array
approaches.idea: Run Kadane's Algorithm to find the maximum subarray sum. Simultaneously compute the minimum subarray sum using a modified Kadane. If all numbers are negative, return the normal maximum. Otherwise, return the larger of normal maximum and totalSum minus minimum subarray sum.
approaches.intuition: The maximum circular subarray either does not wrap (Kadane) or wraps around. Wrapping excludes the minimum subarray, so totalSum - minSum gives the wrapped maximum.
algorithm: Find minimum subarray using modified Kadane.
If maximum sum is negative, return it.
approaches.dryRun:
nums=[5,-3,5]
Total=7
MaxKadane=7
MinKadane=-3
Circular=7-(-3)=10
Answer=max(7,10)=10

visualization.description: The answer is either a normal maximum subarray or a wrapped subarray.

interviewFollowUps: Why is the all-negative case special?
Can Kadane be modified for minimum sum?
Can this be solved using prefix sums?
How would you return the actual circular subarray?
interviewTips: State O(n) time and O(1) space.

cheatSheet.keyObservation: A wrapped maximum subarray equals the total array sum minus the minimum subarray.
cheatSheet.algorithm: Kadane Max → Kadane Min → Total Sum → Compare.

importantPoints: Two possible answers: wrapped and non-wrapped.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to calculate the minimum subarray.
Returning total-minSum when all numbers are negative.
